summary: Frontend TOTP MFA flow end-to-end tested with pyotp-generated codes. Found 1 CRITICAL bug: MfaCard component was defined but never rendered in Account page. Applied 1-line wiring fix (added <MfaCard c={c}/> to the render tree). After fix, all 7 flow steps passed: initial badge Disabled -> Enable 2FA -> QR + secret + code input rendered -> Confirm with valid TOTP -> 10 recovery codes shown + badge flips to Enabled -> Logout -> Login (email+password) requires mfa-code-field -> Login with TOTP navigates to /app -> Disable via window.prompt(TOTP) -> badge back to Disabled.

ui_bugs.issue: MfaCard was defined at line 70 but never included in the JSX render of Account(). Made the 2FA feature completely inaccessible from the UI. Fixed by inserting <MfaCard c={c} /> between the Password card and Preferences card.

issues: Uses native window.prompt() for TOTP code on disable. Works but is inconsistent with the app's dark cyber UI (rest of app uses styled inputs). Consider replacing with an inline styled input / modal.

action_items: Confirm the MfaCard wiring fix in /app/frontend/src/pages/Account.jsx is retained.
(Optional polish) Replace window.prompt in MfaCard.disable() with a styled inline input for consistency with the FrameForge design system.
critical_code_review_comments: Account.jsx: MfaCard was orphaned (defined but never mounted). Basic smoke test after implementing a new card would have caught this - suggests missing manual verification step before handoff.
MfaCard.disable() uses window.prompt() - not testable via standard selectors and blocks the JS thread; consider a controlled input instead.
seed_data_creation: None. Admin MFA was enabled then disabled during test; final state is mfa_enabled=false as required.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin account is left with MFA disabled. To re-test MFA flow: use pyotp.TOTP(secret_from_data-testid=mfa-secret).now(). For disable step, use page.once('dialog', lambda d: asyncio.create_task(d.accept(code))) before clicking mfa-disable-btn (window.prompt). Beware TOTP reuse-protection: wait ~31s between enable and login-with-code if the same 30s window is still active.
